title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
summary: Linus Lee discusses notational intelligence as how the ways we write ideas down shape our thinking. He explores what makes good notation including abstraction and suggestiveness, then proposes that deep learning models might invent entirely new notations and serve as simulators for conceptual frameworks.
writeup: Linus Lee presents notational intelligence: the idea that the systems used to write ideas down shape how thinking happens. The talk identifies properties of good notation, including abstraction and suggestiveness, and uses them to evaluate what makes a notation effective. It then proposes that deep learning models could invent entirely new notations and act as simulators for conceptual frameworks.
